pairs.question: What is Google AI Studio and how does it differ from other AI app builders?
pairs.answer: Google AI Studio is a platform that allows users to build, test, and deploy web applications using AI-powered 'vibe coding' without writing code. Unlike many free-tier alternatives, AI Studio offers native integration with Gemini models, privacy controls where apps remain private by default, and free usage for both creators and users of the apps. It also provides features like database integration and easy sharing via Google Drive links.

pairs.question: How can you use the App Gallery in Google AI Studio to learn or build new apps?
pairs.answer: The App Gallery in Google AI Studio showcases pre-built apps that you can remix, explore, or study. You can click 'code' to view the app's source code, learn implementation details, or copy-paste it for further modification. To build your own version, click 'remix' to create a private copy you can edit. For example, you could remix the 'Comic Book Creator' app to customize it using only free-tier models.

pairs.question: What are the key benefits of using Google AI Studio for privacy-sensitive projects?
pairs.answer: Google AI Studio ensures your apps and prototypes remain strictly private by default, unlike many free-tier competitors where projects are public. This is ideal for sensitive client work, personal ideas, or experimentation without public exposure. Sharing is controlled via Google Drive permissions (e.g., 'anyone with the link'), and only users with access can view or interact with your app.

pairs.question: How do you start vibe coding in Google AI Studio to build a web app?
pairs.answer: To start vibe coding, open the [Build](https://ai.studio/build) section in AI Studio, describe your app in a prompt (e.g., "Build a to-do list app with a clean UI and Firebase database"), and hit enter. The AI agent will generate the UI and logic, which may take up to 5 minutes. You can then iteratively add features by chatting with the code assistant on the left. For example, you can ask to "add a dark mode toggle and a delete button for tasks."

pairs.question: Can Google AI Studio create apps with persistent data storage? If so, how?
pairs.answer: Yes! Google AI Studio can generate apps with databases. Simply ask the AI agent to create an app with database features (e.g., "Build a grocery list app that saves items between sessions"). The agent will automatically set up Firebase Firestore for data storage, Google account authentication, and user-specific data isolation. For instance, you can request "each user should have their own grocery list" or "allow shared lists with labels." The setup includes all necessary backend configurations without manual intervention.

pairs.question: What advanced input methods does Google AI Studio support for vibe coding?
pairs.answer: Google AI Studio supports multiple input methods for vibe coding: 1) Text prompts (e.g., "Add a search bar to my app"), 2) Visual feedback via a drawing canvas to sketch UI changes, and 3) Voice dictation for hands-free updates (e.g., on mobile). The AI agent processes these inputs server-side, so you can close the tab or switch devices mid-task. For example, you could dictate, "Make the login button blue and centered," and the agent will update the UI accordingly.

pairs.question: How does Google AI Studio compare to Antigravity in terms of use cases?
pairs.answer: Google AI Studio and Antigravity are both vibe coding tools, but AI Studio excels in privacy (private apps by default), native Gemini integration, and seamless sharing via Google Drive. Antigravity may offer different model access or workflows. Use AI Studio for confidential prototypes, client work, or projects where sharing is controlled. Antigravity might be preferable for open-source or collaborative projects where public visibility is acceptable. AI Studio is also better for full-stack apps with databases, while Antigravity may focus more on frontend prototyping.
